description: I do AIA billing for several different clients that use Procore and am completely satisfied with everything that I have used. I have worked with Procore for over eight years.
author: Vickie R.
pros: I really liked that it is extremely user-friendly with a short learning curve. There are also video instructions to help with any questions so you don't have to wait for someone to help you.
cons: It is a little pricey, but if you have enough clients, it is well worth the extra cost. There are other alternatives, but if you can afford it, I don't think there is another product out there that can match it.

review_title: Versatile and customizable yet still easy to use
description: We've had a very positive experience with Procore. The support can be reached almost immediately using the chat and they know their stuff. The training videos are comprehensive and set up our new employees for success when using Procore.
author: Sharon H.
pros: Everyone associated with our projects (owners, design team, subcontractors, and our team) has easily accessible access to the project information at any time from anywhere. The system is highly customizable while still being easy to use for the end users. The ability to apply granular permissions when we need someone to have a little more access than others is something I don't see often.
cons: I've always wished we could create reports ourselves and they just debuted this feature. So, nothing that I can think of right now!

description: Not bad. Not as good as ACC. Financial management needs to improve. I think the PCO step and Change Event step are redundant and one should be eliminated which would make the system a lot more efficient on user level.
author: Travis J.
pros: I like the submittal and RFI features in Procore. It's really easy to sort quickly which is helpful walking on the jobsite.
cons: Financial management is convoluted and requires too many steps. If a user has to edit an older change order for some reason, all subsequent change order statuses need to be edited which is a big waste of productive time.

description: Procore is best suited for big General Contractor who has to manage multiple jobs and multiple sub-contractors at the same time. Not useful for small company and beginners.
author: BISHNU A.
pros: Most: Ability to track the construction progress from inception to end. I worked as submittal manager in one of the Company and we used Procore as the main tools. So, it was easy to manage Submittals, RFIs, transmittals, Inspection, Punch list and milestones in Procore.
cons: Cons: It is not for a small company and a beginner. As a beginner, you get overwhelmed by the multiple features that Procore has. Price is costly for a small company

review_title: Life changer for construction projects management
description: We used Procore for several projects throughout its entire duration and it was a success. The entire team found it easy to use from the beginning and we managed to maintain the documentation in order, which helped the closeout process.
author: Favio R.
pros: The feature that i like the most in Procore is how it beautifully integrates multiple file formats in a construction project. You can view MS Project or P6 schedules, open DWG drawings and specifications. Also the RFI and submittals management is excellent. I specially like the versatility on how can you use it, as you can use it in a mobile device and upload pictures, linking them to a specific task, RFI or punch list item.
cons: Some users may need to customize the interface to their specific needs and workflows, and Procore is a little rigid when it comes to customization.

description: We still use ProCore for project management, but not for estimating. It's a great package overall, but the cost justification was just too great.
author: John B.
pros: Procore is a very comprehensive construction management package. It has it all - project management, document management and a fantastic estimating toolbox.
cons: The price - Procore is by far, the most expensive solution that we reviewed. While it more than ticked the tickboxes in terms of functionality, the value for money just wasn't there and ultimately, we went with another solution.

review_title: Comprehensive construction management tool - with a steep learning curve
description: Overall, Procore has been an essential tool in managing construction projects (contracts, in my case) more efficiently. It consolidates all aspects of project management into one platform, which reduces miscommunication and keeps everyone on the same page. The robust features and mobile access are huge benefits, although it does take some time to learn all its functions.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Its comprehensive project management capabilities for construction. The platform centralizes communication, document management, and scheduling, making it easy to collaborate across teams. The mobile app is especially helpful for accessing real-time updates and tracking project progress on-site. Procore’s ability to manage RFIs, submittals, and change orders in one place has improved our workflow efficiency.
cons: What I like least is that it can be a bit overwhelming for new users. Some functions require multiple steps, and the system can feel slow at times. Additionally, the cost is higher compared to other tools, which may be a drawback for smaller projects.

description: It is nice that you can find everything you need for a specific project all in one app. The owner can also interface with you, which is great for change orders and or product selections.
author: Karic R.
pros: I like how you can use the app on your phone when your out on a job site. You can even upload pictures.
cons: It takes a lots of training to get use to it, espacially if you are using several differents modules.

review_title: Highly Recommend for any Construction Company
description: Great experience, work flow is efficient and the grab & down feature is very time saving.
author: Susan V.
pros: Very user friendly, easy to learn & apply to work environment
cons: You are unable to create a CCO from a CE that has more than one line item. This may just have to do with our company as it will not interface with Intacct.

description: Overall is better than 60% b/c there are big improvements to be made after you are committed to the product.
author: Vicky D.
pros: I like the commitment & contract writing with the ability to have current attachments that are project related automatically synced.
cons: The customer service response & the knowledge should be approved upon especially for the price that this paid.

description: Procore has improved our speed, efficiency, and has helped make doing business way easier than before. Since most companies we work with use Procore, it's an invaluable tool to have to keep track of everything we do!
author: Garrett U.
pros: Procore has completely changed how we do business with GC's. From my end, I love the real time updates I get when drawings are updated and how easy it is to follow RFIs that may affect us in the future. The submittals function is also a huge plus for us so we can easily keep track of our submittals and the notifications we get when they have been approved.
cons: The one thing that can be improved upon the the compare drawings feature. Currently, the mobile version is much better at this but when I'm using the desktop version, It is not always accurate and I tend to have to check them by hand.

review_title: Procore brings accountability to all jobsites.
description: We’ve had better documentation with Procore that allowed us to resolve disagreement on scope and timing without the back-and-forth.
author: Ben T.
pros: Procore is great for managing Subcontractors and tracking in real time with photo upload. Because the inspection checklists can help keep teams compliant with local building codes.
cons: It’s a sledgehammer for smaller projects and is a pain to onboard new subcontractors to it without training.

description: Easy interface and easy to learn! Not too much of a learning curve and the design is very user friendly. Overall I have had a great experience with Procore and it is the future of construction management!
author: sami A.
pros: Very easy and organized. No more paper copies or giant folders!!!
cons: Sometimes I have some glitching and don't receive notifications when I should. Other than that easy and nice to use everyday!

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy to use and extremely good at simplifying processes. The collaboration is especially helpful as this is often a difficult element in construction
cons: It can be difficult to get various parties to get involved initially. Construction folks are reluctant to have to "do more work" than they already have to. Asking people to take on a new piece of technology is like pulling teeth.

author: Mark W.
pros: What really helps is that you can get stuff updated live and tag people so nothing gets missed. Also like that you don’t need to chase emails, everything’s just in one spot. I open it even from my phone and it’s all there.
cons: First few days I had no clue where to click. Way too many menus. I was just guessing and hoping I didn’t break something.

review_title: Everything you need in one spot with Procore
description: My overall experience with Procore has been positive. It’s a comprehensive platform that has greatly streamlined our project management processes. From tracking project progress to collaborating with teams, Procore keeps everything in one place, making it easier to stay organized and efficient. Despite the initial learning curve, once you are familiar with the system, it’s an invaluable tool for managing construction projects.
author: Jamie G.
pros: Procore has all aspects of the project centralized into one platform so I can plan everything from a-z in one spot.
cons: One downside to Procore is that it can feel a bit overwhelming at first due to the wide range of features and tools it offers. The initial learning curve can be steep, especially for those unfamiliar with construction management software. Additionally, some customization options for reports and workflows could be more flexible.

description: Overall i liked it and i am deciding if its the right option for us, i do believe i may be siging up.
author: Melissa L.
pros: This sofware is very easy to use and has alot of great features to complete all your construction needs.
cons: There is not way to access your account unless you pay so once the free trial is over if you dont subscribe you can no loger access it
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: I have been able to successfully complete many commercial and industrial projects with multiple disciplines, and coordination was made easy using this program.
author: Colby F.
pros: Procore is a well organized, efficient platform with all of the capabilities you could ever need to preform large scale industrial projects all the way down to a simple residential construction.
cons: They do not have an application for computers, so you are forced to use the website.

description: We have had great success using Procore as a company. Being able to gradually add access to tools has helped our company scale up.
author: Ian S.
pros: Procore is extremely flexible and works on a multitude of scales.
cons: Tools can have almost too many functions. Permissions are hard to manage.

description: Has made the entire construction process seamless and much easier to track.
author: Jon A.
pros: easy of use and depth of integration. One stop shop
cons: Constant changes and updates. Steepens the learning curve when things are changing soo often

review_title: The bigger the job, the better it is.
description: Great! It assists us to lower our email clutter and submit our documents to the GC.
author: Matthew S.
pros: Provides one pane of glass for Subcontractors to communicate with GC's. A centralized are for submittals, Change orders, etc.
cons: Feature heavy and medium learning curve. It's great that we can use it for every project, but smaller projects don't need most of the features.

description: great there's not much more to say ! We love it.
author: gabriel B.
pros: The tracking of projects and the ease of use.
cons: Its slow alot of times when you try to open it up on desktop.

description: I use it to keep track of projects we bid on, it stores a lot of helpful information for projects.
author: Cardi G.
pros: It is easy to navigate and keeps everything organized/easy to locate when searching
cons: Some projects require further allowed access, so getting approval for access is just an extra step that sometimes slows down the process.

description: It was the way that makes everything easier for me and other project teams.
author: Dorila M.
pros: In my previous job Procore was an essential support for my duties.
cons: I love everything about it because I organized all photos and Daily report.

review_title: A shared collaboration tool - Procore insightful article
description: So I used procore at Builder.ai, when our project was initially setup, at a fast paced environment this product turned useful in collaboration with our team , contractors, stakeholders and clients. This turned out to be useful as it has an effective way to insight into the business and automate processes
author: Alfiya M.
pros: I like the way it integrates the full time employees with the contractors along with the stakeholders of the project and the business. Very insightful, detailed and effective tool
cons: The costing is high, initial use requires some training which again involves cost
